title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs bounded worker pool, origin fairness, cancellation, and shutdown from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Concurrency Budget · changed case · Review Feed Go Concurrency Budg…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 6222 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Shutdown Join: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 2079 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stop claims, cancel fetches, bound drain time, join workers, finalize or abandon leases safely, close pools, and report incomplete work.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Shutdown Join.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Shutdown Join, keep Feed Go Shutdown Join connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop claims, cancel fetches, bound drain time, join workers, finalize or abandon leases safely, close pools, and report incomplete work.

At Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Shutdown Join, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 2079 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stop claims, cancel fetches, bound drain time, join workers, finalize or abandon leases safely, close pools, and report incomplete work.

For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Shutdown Join, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Canceling the root context proves every goroutine and database operation stopped.
